NEW The King is following in the footsteps of his late mother in becoming only the second British monarch in history to address Congress . He is also set to make a pointed rejoinder to President Trump's scathing dismissal of the UK's armed forces, particularly the Royal Navy, gently pointing out his own proud history in the service.

The President noted that Mary Anne MacLeod Trump, who died in 2000, 'loved the royal family and the Queen', and that she thought the then-Prince Charles was 'cute'. 'Any time the queen was involved in a ceremony or anything, my mother would be glued to the television and she would say, "look, Donald, look how beautiful that is",' Trump said. 'She really did love the family. But I also remember her saying very clearly: "Charles, look, young Charles, he is so cute".'

Children should ALWAYS be the focus and it's a sign of a strong marriage to work with each other on managing the impact. Harrowing news details have emerged of the extreme punishments meted out in North Korea, including prisoners beaten to death with a hammer, a pregnant woman executed, and a farm manager shot after angering Kim Jong Un. In some of the most shocking cases, inmates were killed in secret 'indoor executions' using blunt weapons, while defectors said they witnessed  the shooting of pregnant women and even minors despite official claims such punishments are banned. One man was reportedly executed after all the baby terrapins at a state-run farm died, with sources saying he was shot dead shortly after Kim berated officials during a visit.

Residents living next door on Ifield Road have engaged in a prolonged war against their neighbour, claiming the house is 'rotting from the inside out' and has become home to multiple pests. And several years ago, a rotting corpse was found in the basement after residents complained about a smell and leaking drains previously turned the inside into a swamp. Now, Kensington and Chelsea council has moved to force Nicholas Halbritter (pictured) to confront long-running issues at his property in west London by handing him a Section 215 order. This is a legal power enabling local planning authorities to compel owners to tidy up land or buildings that adversely affect local amenity.

The UAE is the world's seventh-largest oil supplier, and in the medium term, significant downward pressure on prices is likely due to the additional, no longer cartel-bound supply. The UAE is explicitly leaving the cartel to gain more flexibility and to utilize its enormous production capacity from currently unused reserves. The stated goal appears to be to gradually increase production to 5 million barrels per day. If this additional oil finds its way onto the world market, it will naturally drive down prices. Whether this will happen remains to be seen. It will depend primarily on how well the UAE manages to protect its transport routes.

As of May 1, the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) will have lost one of its key members, weakening its leverage over global oil supplies and prices.
  • 2 comments
  • share UAE QUITS OPEC weakening oil cartel's leverage over global prices

Poor Rachel, taken away from her family so cruelly. May she rest in eternal peace. 🙏

This is the astonishing moment a three-year-old boy chatted with his father about the knife-wielding murderer who killed his mother in front of him in a London park. Rachel Nickell, 23, was stabbed 49 times on Wimbledon Common while walking her dog Molly with her son Alex on July 15, 1992 in a crime that shocked the nation. Miss Nickell was also sexually assaulted by the attacker before he fled - and Alex was found by a passer-by next to his mother, repeatedly saying: 'Wake up, Mummy.' Now, previously-unseen home video which features in a new Netflix documentary - revealed exclusively by the Daily Mail this morning - has shown Alex speaking to his father André Hanscombe about the horror he had witnessed when he was aged two.

A bright green parcel locker that was abruptly 'plonked' on a quiet cul-de-sac has been removed after less than a month following outrage from residents. Homeowners on Arne Road in Walsgrave, Coventry, were 'up in arms' after the 8ft-tall delivery locker was dropped on their doorsteps on April 10. The battery-run, solar-powered delivery point is owned by Yeep!, which has since admitted the location was 'wholly unsuitable' and removed the locker yesterday afternoon. The delivery point had been placed half a mile from the nearest shop, with locals claiming they hadn't seen the parcel lockers used once since it was installed.
  • 26 comments
  • share Parcel locker 'plonked' on quiet street removed following 'outrage'

Harrowing images from inside a high-speed train have been released for the first time, capturing the final moments before a derailment that killed 46 people in Spain. The pictures, included in a Civil Guard report now submitted to a judge in Montoro, show the impact of tearing through the rear carriages just before the lights went out.  The dossier also documents the precise moment the Iryo service left the tracks near Adamuz, in Córdoba province, after a rail fractured - triggering a catastrophic chain of events that forced an oncoming Alvia train into an emergency stop.

Lisa Kudrow says the Friends writing team were 'mostly men' who 'stayed up late at night to discuss their sexual fantasies about Jennifer Aniston and Courteney Cox'

Friends star Lisa Kudrow has accused the show's predominantly male writing team of openly discussing their sexual fantasies about her female co-stars. The actress became a household name thanks to her starring role as ditzy masseuse Phoebe Buffay in the sitcom, featuring in every episode during its ten-year run.
